scenarios.title: /trends MapTrendEntryAsync populates RefreshPolicyDistribution from fraction columns when reader rows carry them
scenarios.rationale: MapTrendEntryAsync is the seam where the SQL reader becomes a wire-shape DailyTrendEntry. Any miss on a fraction ordinal — wrong column name, wrong order, type mismatch — surfaces here. This is the primary contract test for the new column triple on /trends.

scenarios.title: /trends rounds fractions to 1 decimal place to match /summary precision contract
scenarios.rationale: The mapping uses Math.Round(value, 1) so /trends and /summary cannot disagree by floating-point noise. Without the rounding, a daily fraction emitted as 0.333333... would mismatch the /summary aggregate (which sums then rounds) and break dashboard reconciliation.

scenarios.title: NULL fraction column produces 0.0 in the DailyTrendEntry, never throws
scenarios.rationale: IsDBNullAsync guards each fraction column. If a pre-PR1 row reaches this code path with NULL fractions, the mapping must default to 0.0 — not throw and not skip the trend day. The default keeps daily entries dense even across the pre/post-PR1 boundary.

scenarios.title: SUM(COALESCE(fraction_*, 0)) in the /trends query shields the daily aggregate from pre-PR rows
scenarios.rationale: Same pattern as the /summary endpoint — pre-PR rows have NULL fractions; without COALESCE the daily SUM propagates NULL and every historical day in the window returns null fractions on the wire.

scenarios.title: DailyTrendEntry exposes RefreshPolicyDistribution as part of the /trends wire contract
scenarios.rationale: The new property on DailyTrendEntry is the public API surface for /trends consumers. Missing or null distributions break the dashboard that expects every daily entry to carry the field.

scenarios.title: MapTrendEntryAsync is extracted as an internal static method consuming DbDataReader + CancellationToken for mocked-reader tests
scenarios.rationale: The extracted method shape (internal static, DbDataReader + CT parameters) is what makes the mapping unit-testable. A refactor that moves the body back inline or changes the signature breaks every existing mapping test without touching mapping behaviour.
